figures.caption: Figure 4: Online accuracy on label-permuted EMNIST. FADE+SGD and FADE+Adam apply FADE to the final layer.
Figure 6: Online accuracy with partial label permutations.

Table 10: Impact of FADE’s adaptive decay on partial label-permuted EMNIST (24 stable classes which never change labels) with Adam. Results are average online accuracy ± standard deviation across 5 seeds, with step size α = 0.0001. θλ = 0 corresponds to fixed weight decay on the head.
Figure 2: MSE by group on nonlinear tracking problem. With γ0 = −9.2(λ0 ≈ 0.0001), comparing FADE+SGD (θλ = 2) with its fixed-decay counterpart FADE+SGD (θλ = 0).
